reasoning: As of May 30, 2025, 8 out of the 10 inmates who escaped on May 16 have been recaptured, representing an 80% success rate within the first two weeks of the manhunt. The remaining two fugitives, Antoine Massey and Derrick Groves, are subject to an intensified search involving local, state, and federal authorities (including the US Marshals). A substantial reward of $50,000 per inmate was announced on May 29, which significantly increases the likelihood of public tips leading to their capture. Furthermore, law enforcement has proactively dismantled their support network, with at least 13 accomplices already arrested, including a jail worker. While Antoine Massey has a history of prior escapes, he was notably recaptured on the same day during his 2019 escape after fleeing to Texas. With 32 days remaining until the July 1 deadline, the high intensity of the search, the significant financial incentive for informants, and the historical pattern of recapturing these specific individuals suggest a high probability that all 10 will be in custody or otherwise accounted for.
